req.title: Bill pay
req.statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.
preconditions: biller registered

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-007 defines a bill-pay endpoint with a closed numeric range [$1, $50000]. Test design applies: EP for a nominal mid-range amount (positive); BVA at the exact lower bound $1 and upper bound $50000 (boundary); NEG for amounts outside the range (negative). A DT case covers the settlement-date logic conditioned on biller cut-off state. STC traces the lifecycle: unscheduled -> scheduled -> confirmed. Together these cover the statement, error pathway (422), and settlement-date determinism.

tests.name: test_billpay_nominal_amount_returns_200_and_settlement_date
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and session token is valid
Biller 'BILLER-001' is registered in the system
Customer account has sufficient funds
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains 'settlement_date' as a valid ISO-8601 date string
trace: EP: $500 is within the valid equivalence class [$1, $50000]
tests.executable:
import pytest
import re

def test_billpay_nominal_amount_returns_200_and_settlement_date(
    api_client, registered_biller, funded_account
):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "payment_id" in body, "Missing payment_id"
    assert "settlement_date" in body, "Missing settlement_date"
    iso_pattern = r"^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$"
    assert re.match(iso_pattern, body["settlement_date"]), (
        f"settlement_date not ISO-8601: {body['settlement_date']}"
    )
    assert body.get("status") == "SCHEDULED", f"Unexpected status: {body.get('status')}"

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and session token is valid
Biller 'BILLER-001' is registered in the system
actions: POST /billpay with amount=$0.99 (below $1 lower bound), biller_id=BILLER-001
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing amount validation
trace: R-FIN-007: error pathway — 422 when amount out of range
NEG: amount $0.99 is in the invalid partition below $1
tests.executable:
def test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422(
    api_client, registered_biller, funded_account
):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "amount": "0.99",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "amount" in error_text, "Error detail must reference 'amount'"

tests.name: test_billpay_settlement_date_differs_by_biller_cutoff
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and session token is valid
Biller 'BILLER-EARLY' has cut-off time 12:00 UTC
Biller 'BILLER-LATE' has cut-off time 20:00 UTC
Request is submitted at a time between 12:01 and 19:59 UTC so results differ
Customer account has sufficient funds
expected: settlement_date for BILLER-EARLY is at least 1 calendar day later than BILLER-LATE settlement_date
Decision table: (submitted after early cut-off) -> next-business-day; (submitted before late cut-off) -> same-business-day
trace: R-FIN-007: deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off
DT: two conditions (cut-off passed / not passed) produce different settlement dates
tests.executable:
from datetime import date

def test_billpay_settlement_date_differs_by_biller_cutoff(
    api_client, funded_account,
    biller_early_cutoff, biller_late_cutoff,
    freeze_time_between_cutoffs
):
    def pay(biller_id):
        resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json={
            "biller_id": biller_id,
            "account_id": funded_account["id"],
            "amount": "100.00",
            "currency": "USD"
        })
        assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
        return resp.json()["settlement_date"]

    early_date = date.fromisoformat(pay(biller_early_cutoff["id"]))
    late_date = date.fromisoformat(pay(biller_late_cutoff["id"]))
    diff = (early_date - late_date).days
    assert diff >= 1, (
        f"Expected early-cutoff biller to settle later; "
        f"early={early_date}, late={late_date}"
    )

tests.name: test_billpay_state_transition_unscheduled_to_scheduled
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and session token is valid
Biller 'BILLER-001' is registered in the system
No prior pending bill payment exists for this biller+account pair
actions: POST /billpay with amount=$250.00, biller_id=BILLER-001 to trigger transition
expected: POST returns HTTP 200 or 201 with status='SCHEDULED'
GET returns status='SCHEDULED' confirming persisted state transition
payment_id is stable across both calls
trace: STC: state machine — NONE -> SCHEDULED triggered by customer submit
tests.executable:
def test_billpay_state_transition_unscheduled_to_scheduled(
    api_client, registered_biller, funded_account
):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller["id"],
        "account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "amount": "250.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    create_resp = api_client.post("/billpay", json=payload)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = create_resp.json()
    payment_id = body.get("payment_id")
    assert payment_id, "payment_id missing in create response"
    assert body.get("status") == "SCHEDULED"

    fetch_resp = api_client.get(f"/billpay/{payment_id}")
    assert fetch_resp.status_code == 200
    fetched = fetch_resp.json()
    assert fetched.get("status") == "SCHEDULED"
    assert fetched.get("payment_id") == payment_id
